title: plantaginaceae
intro: The taxonomical rank Plantaginaceae is a family of flowering plants. It is a monophyletic group of plants that includes species commonly known as plantains, foxgloves, and toadflaxes, among others. This family is characterized by its diverse range of species, many of which are popular garden ornamentals. Plantaginaceae comprises around 90 genera and over 1,000 species of plants.
